Now I see the structure more clearly:
- Yellow shapes in the right panel:
  - id=12: pixels=16, boundary at (7, 44) - this is the top yellow shape (L-shaped)
  - id=45: pixels=14, boundary at (24, 45) - this is the bottom yellow shape (square-ish)
  - id=110: pixels=6, boundary at (55, 20) - yellow dot in the gray panel
  - id=111: pixels=6, boundary at (57, 8) - yellow dot in the blue panel
- Dark squares: Many of them, forming a checkerboard pattern in the right panel and a single one in the left panel.
Looking at the image, the right panel has a checkerboard pattern with dark squares (c color) and two yellow shapes. The left panel has a gray background with one dark square.
The two small panels at the bottom left have:
- Blue panel (left): contains yellow dot (id=111)
- Gray panel (right): contains yellow dot (id=110)
I think the goal might be to click on the yellow shapes in the right panel to "collect" them or move them to the panels at the bottom. Let me try clicking on one of the yellow shapes.
Actually, let me reconsider. Looking at level 1, the goal was to click the blue blob. In level 2, the blue blob toggles between left and right. Maybe the goal is different.
Let me look at the two small panels more carefully:
- Blue panel (left): has 2 yellow dots (id=111, but wait, id=111 is at (57, 8) which is in the blue panel)
- Gray panel (right): has 1 yellow dot (id=110 at (55, 20))
Wait, looking at the image again:
- The blue panel at the bottom left has 2 yellow dots
- The gray panel at the bottom left has 1 yellow dot
But the segmentation shows:
- id=110: Y, pixels=6, at (55, 20) - this is in the gray panel (right)
- id=111: Y, pixels=6, at (57, 8) - this is in the blue panel (left)
So the blue panel has 1 yellow dot and the gray panel has 1 yellow dot. But the image shows the blue panel has 2 yellow dots and the gray panel has 1 yellow dot. Let me re-examine.
Actually, looking at the image more carefully, the blue panel has 2 yellow dots (one at the top, one at the bottom), and the gray panel has 1 yellow dot. But the segmentation only shows one yellow dot per panel. Maybe the dots are connected and form one object?
Let me try clicking on the yellow shapes in the right panel to see what happens.
